run_summary: Screen 1 portfolio is heavily leveraged to the Anthropic Fable cybersecurity model catalyst (6 positions, 5 from same trigger). OMCL, CGNX, PLAB positions are 1 day old with mixed P&L; AMBA and TENB are 3 days in and slightly underwater. New flags on OMCL, CGNX, PLAB, AMBA repeat the same thesis (Fable has zero overlap with their businesses) and should be consolidated as a basket; HCAT is a new entry point but carries idiosyncratic fundamental stress that weakens the sympathy-fade read.

entries.name: Omnicell, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Anthropic releases Fable model with strict guardrails, drawing cybersecurity researcher pushback
entries.thesis: An Anthropic cybersecurity model has zero overlap with hospital pharmacy automation. Omnicell sells DEA-regulated physical dispensing cabinets and consumable blister packs that require a BAA-bound vendor and physical install — Fable doesn't touch that TAM. The curation false-victim pattern (physical robotics, Schedule II compliance) is corroborated by the filings.
entries.what_kills: An actual customer-concentration shift in the 10-Q, a missed earnings print, or a debt/covenant 8-K — the K already flags substantial debt and SaaS-renewal risk, so a fundamental miss would invalidate the sympathy-fade thesis.
entries.reasoning: Ticker already held in this screen's portfolio (3 days, -6.86%). Screen 1 does not support ADD as a position action; new flags on existing holdings trigger position reviews (HOLD/TRIM/EXIT), not new BUY decisions.

entries.name: Cognex Corporation
entries.catalyst: Anthropic releases Fable model with strict guardrails, drawing cybersecurity researcher pushback
entries.thesis: Anthropic's Fable is a cybersecurity LLM — it has no plausible overlap with deterministic factory-floor machine vision. CGNX's moat is integrator networks, deterministic latency, and an installed base on production lines. The fade is purely thematic 'AI-tech selloff' contagion, compounded by an already-extended weekly drawdown.
entries.what_kills: The 10-K does acknowledge open-source/AI vision as a real future risk, so a credible competing release (e.g. a Google/NVIDIA vision platform aimed at industrial QA) within the window would shift this from minimal to indirect. Also watch for a guidance cut from large auto/EV customer slowdown.
entries.reasoning: Ticker already held in this screen's portfolio (1 day, +5.49%). New flag confirms thesis but does not trigger a new BUY decision.

entries.name: Photronics, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Anthropic releases Fable model with strict guardrails, drawing cybersecurity researcher pushback
entries.thesis: Photronics is structurally a beneficiary of AI capex (more chips → more masks), and Fable is a cybersecurity model with no semiconductor or capex implication. The selloff is mechanically backwards: a cyber-model release does not reduce TSMC/Samsung mask demand. Curation hypothesis is well-supported.
entries.what_kills: A genuine semis-capex guidance cut from a major foundry, or a tariff/export-control headline that hits PLAB's China operations (a real risk per the K).
entries.reasoning: Ticker already held in this screen's portfolio (1 day, +8.76%). New flag corroborates thesis but does not trigger a new BUY decision.

entries.name: Ambarella, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Anthropic releases Fable model with strict guardrails, drawing cybersecurity researcher pushback
entries.thesis: Ambarella is an edge-AI inference beneficiary, and Fable is a cloud cybersecurity LLM with no edge-inference overlap. The curation note ('direct AI beneficiary') is supported. However, AMBA already had a weak 5-day tape, so part of this is broader semis fade, not pure Fable sympathy.
entries.what_kills: The K explicitly flags NVIDIA, Qualcomm, Mobileye as competitors with greater resources in automotive AI — a competitive release from one of those (not Anthropic) would be the real threat. Also customer concentration (WT = ~70% of revenue, Insta360 large end-customer) is a real fundamental risk.
entries.reasoning: Ticker already held in this screen's portfolio (3 days, -2.97%). New flag is at confidence 3 (below the re-review threshold) and threat_assessment is 'minimal' not 'none,' so it adds uncertainty rather than conviction. Existing position review (HOLD) supersedes new entry.

entries.name: Health Catalyst, Inc
entries.catalyst: Anthropic releases Fable model with strict guardrails, drawing cybersecurity researcher pushback
entries.thesis: Anthropic Fable is a cybersecurity model with no overlap with HIPAA-bound provider data warehousing and EHR-integrated analytics. However, HCAT has real idiosyncratic stress (Project Nexus 9% RIF in Q2 2026, DOS-to-Ignite migration churn) that means this move could partly reflect ongoing fundamental concerns rather than pure sympathy.
entries.what_kills: The 10-Q explicitly flags continuing DOS-Ignite churn and revenue decline — if a sell-side note tied today's move to fundamentals rather than the Fable headline, the 'sympathy fade' read is wrong.
entries.reasoning: Confidence 3 (at min_buy_confidence threshold) and threat_assessment is 'minimal' — borderline for BUY. Critical weakness: filings explicitly flag 'Project Nexus 9% RIF in Q2 2026' and 'DOS-to-Ignite migration churn,' meaning today's -6.43% move may reflect idiosyncratic fundamentals (DOS-Ignite revenue decline) rather than pure sympathy fade on a cybersecurity model. Panic_calibration is 'unjustified' IF the move is purely Fable-driven, but the restructuring and 10-Q churn signals complicate the diagnosis. Five-day move +20.3% contradicts the 1-day -6.43%, suggesting volatility and uncertainty. WATCH pending clearer separation of Fable sympathy from Project Nexus impact.
